summary: Energy (XLE) is the dominant leader as a severe geopolitical supply shock in the Middle East drives oil toward $120, while the broader market rotates into defensives (XLU, XLP) and out of high-duration Tech (XLK) as the 10Y yield hits 4.44%. The market is grappling with a 'bear steepener' yield curve that reflects persistent inflation fears and a hawkish Fed pause.

current_leadership.explanation: XLE is riding a massive geopolitical risk premium due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. XLK and XLY are lagging as the 10Y Treasury yield (4.44%) forces a valuation reset on growth stocks, while XLF is underperforming despite a steepening curve due to rising credit risks and a slowing labor market (4.4% unemployment).

outlook_1_4_weeks: Expect continued momentum in Energy and Materials as long as Middle East tensions remain at a boiling point. Tactical interest in Utilities (XLU) and Staples (XLP) will likely increase as investors hedge against a potential 'hard landing' triggered by the energy price spike. Watch for Q1 earnings previews where Tech guidance will be scrutinized for AI-driven margin resilience.
outlook_1_3_months: The medium-term pivot depends on the 10Y yield; if it stabilizes above 4.5%, the rotation out of Tech will deepen. However, if diplomatic de-escalation occurs, a sharp mean-reversion trade could favor oversold XLK and XLY. Monitor the Fed's June meeting for any shift in the 'one cut only' 2026 projection.

scenarios.name: Energy Supercycle / Conflict Escalation
scenarios.analysis: Continued disruption in the Strait of Hormuz keeps oil above $110, driving headline inflation toward 4%. This 'tax on the consumer' crushes Discretionary and Industrials, while Energy and inflation-hedged Materials outperform.

scenarios.name: Diplomatic De-escalation / Yield Stabilization
scenarios.analysis: A breakthrough in US-Iran negotiations leads to a rapid $20-30 drop in crude. This allows the 10Y yield to retreat toward 4.0%, triggering a massive 'relief rally' in high-duration growth and financials.

watch_items: Strait of Hormuz shipping status and insurance premiums
April 28-29 FOMC meeting rhetoric on 'transitory' energy shocks
Q1 Earnings reports from JPMorgan (May 14) and NVIDIA
